primary_accounting_issue: Eligibility of intra-group dividend receivables for hedge accounting designation on foreign exchange risk in consolidated financial statements

primary_authority.reason: Directly governs hedge accounting eligibility by restricting hedged items to transactions with external parties only
Defines what can qualify as a hedged item (assets, liabilities, firm commitments, or highly probable forecast transactions)
Clarifies that hedge accounting can only be applied to foreign exchange differences with a party external to the reporting entity

supporting_authority.reason: Provides the only exception for intragroup items, limited to foreign currency risk of intragroup monetary items where the risk affects consolidated profit or loss

peripheral_authority.reason: Addresses cash flow presentation of interest and dividends but does not govern hedge accounting eligibility
Addresses classification of dividends as expense but not hedge accounting restrictions
Requires dividend disclosure but does not affect hedge accounting treatment

treatment_families.family: Hedge accounting not permitted for intra-group items
Hedge accounting permitted only for qualifying intragroup monetary items

approaches.label: No hedge accounting for intra-group dividend receivables
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: Under IFRS 9 paragraph 6.3.5, only transactions with parties external to the reporting entity can be designated as hedged items; intra-group dividends do not meet this requirement in consolidated financial statements

approaches.label: Hedge accounting for intragroup monetary items
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRIC 16 paragraph 6.3.6 provides an exception allowing hedge accounting for intragroup monetary items' foreign currency risk when it affects consolidated profit or loss, though dividend receivables are typically non-monetary until declared
